name: Hamper of Gwyddno
description: The Hamper of Gwyddno Garanhir (Longshanks) is a legendary object from Welsh mythology, known for its seemingly endless capacity to provide food and sustenance. It is described as a basket or hamper that would magically produce any food desired, ensuring that its owner would never go hungry. Gwyddno Garanhir, a figure of some prominence in Welsh tradition, is often associated with the sea and fertile lands. The hamper's powers are said to stem from a blessing or enchantment, allowing it to defy the natural limits of resources. Its significance lies in its representation of abundance, generosity, and the potential for magical provision. However, stories also hint at the Hamper's potential for misuse or loss, suggesting that its magic was not always reliable or controllable. The Hamper is a powerful symbol of the land's bounty and the importance of responsible stewardship of resources. Its legacy persists in Welsh folklore as a reminder of the magical possibilities and the inherent risks associated with unchecked abundance. It is a testament to the power of myth to reflect both the hopes and anxieties of a culture regarding sustenance and prosperity, embodying the delicate balance between fortune and responsibility.
subtitle: The never-empty basket of Welsh legend.
material: Unknown, possibly woven with magical reeds
origin: Gift from a deity or enchanted by a powerful sorcerer.
currentLocation: Lost to time; its whereabouts are unknown.

keyMyths.title: The Hamper's Bounty
keyMyths.description: The Hamper of Gwyddno was said to provide any food requested, ensuring that Gwyddno and his people never suffered from hunger. This magical ability made Gwyddno a generous host and a respected leader.

keyMyths.title: The Hamper's Loss
keyMyths.description: Accounts suggest that the Hamper was eventually lost or stolen, possibly due to misuse or a failure to respect its magical properties. This loss represents the fragility of abundance and the importance of responsible stewardship.

sources.author: John Rhys
sources.description: Provides insight into Welsh folklore and mentions Gwyddno Garanhir.
sources.text: Provides insight into Welsh folklore and mentions Gwyddno Garanhir.

sources.author: Lady Charlotte Guest
sources.description: A collection of Welsh tales, though the Hamper isn't a central focus.
